note: Each entry covers one survey question. Multi-response questions list all sub-variable names in coding_scheme; each sub-variable is binary (0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable).

variables.description: Whether the constitution refers to 'democracy' or 'democratic'
How many times the constitution mentions the words 'democracy' or 'democratic' (asked only if DEMOC=1). Excludes occurrences in the country's official name.
Whether the constitution suggests that citizens have the right to overthrow their government under certain circumstances
Whether the constitution provides for a commission for truth and reconciliation
Whether the constitution mentions anything about crimes committed by the previous regime
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes, these crimes will be pardoned; 2=Yes, these crimes are punishable; 3=No; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine

variables.description: Whether the constitution stipulates that some public office holders take an oath to support or abide by the constitution
Whether the constitution refers to the 'dignity of man' or human 'dignity' (must be explicitly mentioned, not implied)
Whether the constitution refers to 'socialism' or 'socialist'
variables.notes: Borderline; indicates ideological orientation toward state-provided socioeconomic goods

variables.description: Whether the constitution refers to the 'free market,' 'capitalism,' or an analogous term
variables.notes: Borderline; indicates ideological orientation toward market-based economy

variables.description: Whether the constitution provides for at least one procedure for amending the constitution
Whether any parts of the constitution are explicitly unamendable (asked only if AMEND=1)
variables.notes: Unamendable clauses ('eternity clauses') represent the highest form of constitutional rigidity and often protect fundamental rights or democratic structures

variables.description: Who is allowed to propose amendments to the constitution (asked only if AMEND=1). Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): AMNDPROP_1=Head of State; AMNDPROP_2=Head of Government; AMNDPROP_3=Government/Cabinet; AMNDPROP_4=First/only Chamber of Legislature; AMNDPROP_5=Second Chamber; AMNDPROP_6=Both Chambers required; AMNDPROP_7=Subsidiary Units; AMNDPROP_8=Public (initiatives/referendum); AMNDPROP_9=Members of Judiciary; AMNDPROP_10=Ombudsman; AMNDPROP_96=Other; AMNDPROP_97=Unable to Determine; AMNDPROP_98=Not Specified
variables.notes: AMNDPROP_7 (subsidiary units) and AMNDPROP_8 (public) are particularly relevant to federalism and democratic rigidity respectively

variables.description: Who approves amendments to the constitution (asked only if AMEND=1). Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): AMNDAPPR_1=Head of State; AMNDAPPR_2=Head of Government; AMNDAPPR_3=Government/Cabinet; AMNDAPPR_4=First/only Chamber of Legislature; AMNDAPPR_5=Second Chamber; AMNDAPPR_6=Both Chambers required; AMNDAPPR_7=Subsidiary Units; AMNDAPPR_8=Public (referendum); AMNDAPPR_96=Other; AMNDAPPR_97=Unable to Determine; AMNDAPPR_98=Not Specified
variables.notes: AMNDAPPR_7 (subsidiary units) reflects federal veto on amendments; AMNDAPPR_8 (public referendum) adds direct democratic requirement

variables.description: Whether constitutional amendments require more than a simple majority by the legislature to be approved (asked only if legislature is the approving body)
What proportion of the legislative vote is needed to approve a constitutional amendment (asked only if AMNDAMAJ=1)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Absolute Majority of Members; 2=3/5 Majority; 3=2/3 Majority; 4=3/4 Majority; 5=Unspecified supermajority; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable
